provenance: Authored from job_description.docx (Senior AI Engineer — Founding Team, Redrob AI). Declarative WHAT only: criteria + evidence bindings + concept terms + narratives. No numeric weights/thresholds/formulas (those live in Goals 4/5). Human-reviewed; no build/runtime LLM dependency.

model.name: BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5

criteria.description: Production experience with embeddings-based retrieval deployed to real users (handled drift, index refresh, retrieval-quality regression).

criteria.provenance: JD 'Things you absolutely need': vector DB / hybrid search infra ops.

criteria.provenance: JD 'How to read between the lines': shipped end-to-end ranking/search/rec at scale at a product company.

ideal_narrative: Senior applied ML engineer with about seven years of experience, four to five of them building machine learning systems at product companies rather than services firms. I shipped an end-to-end search and recommendation ranking system to real users at scale: I built the embeddings-based retrieval layer, ran the vector index and hybrid search infrastructure in production, and owned how we measured ranking quality. I handled embedding drift, index refresh, and retrieval-quality regressions. I designed the evaluation framework end to end, tracking NDCG, MRR and MAP offline and correlating them with online A/B engagement. Strong Python and code quality. I have opinions about hybrid versus dense retrieval, offline versus online evaluation, and when to fine-tune versus prompt an LLM, grounded in systems I actually built and operated.
ideal_narrative_paraphrases: Product-company machine learning engineer who built and operated a candidate and content ranking, search, and recommendation system used by millions. Owned the dense embedding retrieval, the vector search index, hybrid retrieval, and the offline-to-online evaluation loop. Comfortable shipping a working ranker fast and then improving it with rigorous offline metrics and A/B testing. Deep on retrieval and ranking from before it was fashionable; strong Python.

Engineer who owns the intelligence layer of a product: the retrieval, ranking, and matching that decide what users see. Built embeddings retrieval deployed to real users, ran a vector database and hybrid search in production, designed NDCG/MRR/MAP evaluation with offline-online correlation, and tuned relevance from real recruiter and user feedback. Six to eight years, mostly at product companies, scrappy and product-minded.

antiprofiles.label: keyword_stuffer
antiprofiles.narrative: Profile lists every trending AI and machine learning keyword as a skill — RAG, Pinecone, FAISS, embeddings, LLMs, vector search, transformers, recommendation systems — but the job title and actual work are unrelated, for example human resources, marketing, content writing, or operations. The career history and assessments show no evidence of ever building retrieval, ranking, or search systems.

antiprofiles.label: research_only
antiprofiles.narrative: Career entirely in academic and research-only roles — PhD researcher, research scientist, postdoc, lab affiliate — publishing papers and prototypes with no production deployment, no real users, and no experience shipping or operating a system in production.

antiprofiles.label: cv_speech_robotics
antiprofiles.narrative: Specialist in computer vision, speech, or robotics: object detection, image segmentation, YOLO, OpenCV, speech recognition, text to speech, perception and autonomy. No meaningful natural language processing or information retrieval, ranking, or search experience.

antiprofiles.label: consulting_only
antiprofiles.narrative: Entire career spent at IT services and consulting firms such as TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, Cognizant, and Capgemini, delivering client projects and staffing engagements, with no experience at a product company building and owning a product used by real users.

antiprofiles.label: recent_llm_only
antiprofiles.narrative: Recent graduate-style AI experience consisting only of the last year of LangChain tutorials and OpenAI API calls wired into demo chatbots, with no earlier machine learning production experience and no understanding of retrieval, ranking, or evaluation before large language models became popular.

stuffer.definition: lexical_concept_score - dense_cosine(ideal): high when a profile keyword-matches concepts but its narrative does not resemble a real practitioner.
